explicit_text: John Devan moved to Miami about eight years ago from Jacksonville, seeking better professional opportunities. He joined a well-known law firm there and has been advancing in his career ever since. Miami, as a southern metropolis of Florida, became the new home for John and his family, where he is also involved in politics and rumored to be a candidate for Governor of Florida in 1940.

explicit_text: About ten years ago, my children encouraged me to move to the Riverside section of Jacksonville, where I bought property on Park Street and joined the Riverside Park Methodist Church. Riverside was a brief chapter in my life, but it showed how much I valued familiar friends and surroundings.

explicit_text: I was born in Gardin, South Carolina, in 1864 and spent my early years there during and after the War between the States. My family was fairly prosperous despite the hardships, and I was sent to school in Columbia to complete my education. South Carolina is where my roots are, and it was from there that my husband and I moved to Florida in 1890.
